anchored_threads.thread_name: Sāṅkhya / first-principles knowledge — substrate antiquity and engineering humility about ownership
anchored_threads.evidence: The verse closes the substrate block with the temporal-precedence axis: the embodied is unborn, eternal, ancient — older than every body it has occupied. The chapter thesis explicitly maps this to 'architectural invariants. What is the system genuinely doing vs. what is the current implementation.' The engineering layer extends this to 'the customer-need predates the codebase, the company, the language' — anchoring on purāṇa as the temporal-antiquity predicate. The block-B closure is correctly placed.

verdict_reason: Verse anchors thread 1 of the chapter and serves as block B's natural closure. The voice is correct (didactic-corrective). The hard edge is preserved (audit ownership language; do not soften into 'humility theater'). The verse's role as substrate-doctrine final-naming is fulfilled. The progression 16 → 17 → 18 → 19 → 20 (cut → name → license → negate symmetric attribution → name as ancient) is doctrinally coherent. PASS.
